name: George W. Bush
description: President of the United States from 2001 to 2009
abstract: George Walker Bush (born July 6, 1946) is an American former politician, businessman, and former United States Air Force officer who was the 43rd president of the United States, serving from 2001 to 2009. A member of the Republican Party, he served as the governor of Texas from 1995 to 2000.

concerts.label: HISTORYTalks™
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Heath
about_quotes.evidence: Speaking of that, when you asked me before, I had a friend who told me that when he made a speech at the democratic convention before, when George Bush got his second, at the Democratic Convention...
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Heath — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jim Hall
about_quotes.evidence: I said, “There’s three things America has given the world.” I said, “Cigarettes, McDonald’s and George Bush.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Jim Hall — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Wendy Oxenhorn
about_quotes.evidence: I remember when George Bush said we were going into Afghanistan and we were going to start to bomb...
about_quotes.doc_title: Wendy Oxenhorn — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Nancy Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: They picked at George Bush. You have to be prepared to take whatever they are sending your way.
about_quotes.doc_title: Nancy Wilson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
